name: Constant
classification_notes: Constant is a sans-serif with grotesque characteristics, featuring a wide weight range from Thin to Heavy and a straightforward upright structure suited to display and text settings.
summary: A seven-weight grotesque sans with variable font support and extensive multilingual coverage across hundreds of Latin-script languages.
description: Spanning Thin to Heavy with a variable font option, Constant offers a straightforward grotesque structure suited to branding, packaging and editorial work. Opentype features include standard and discretionary ligatures, fractions, stylistic alternates for J and R, and a full suite of numeral styles. Language support is unusually broad, covering Vietnamese diacritics and a large catalogue of Latin-script languages from Batak to Welsh.
foundry_blurb: Underscore Type position Constant as suitable for vibrant packaging design, fashion statements and logo and business card use, highlighting its multilingual character set and cosmopolitan reach.

name: Quell
classification_notes: Quell presents as a humanist sans with two distinct stroke-contrast subfamilies: a monolinear cut and a contrasted cut that introduces stroke width variation for a warmer, more organic quality.
summary: A sans-serif in two subfamilies — monolinear and contrasted stroke widths — pitched at identity, signage and editorial work across an extensive language range.
description: Quell splits into Linear and Contrast subfamilies, where the Linear variant keeps strokes at even thickness for direct, imperative settings, and the Contrast variant introduces stroke width variation for a warmer, more expressive register. Both run across six weights from Thin to Extrabold with oblique companions, and a variable font is included. Language coverage is unusually broad, spanning hundreds of Latin-script languages with strong extended diacritic support.
foundry_blurb: Underscore Type describe Quell as a typeface suited to identity, signage and sophisticated styling, with the Linear subfamily suited to direct and imperative messages and the Contrast subfamily offering a warmer, more humane voice for editorial and expressive use.

name: Jozef
classification_notes: The page copy emphasises sharp edges and a hard, clear voice, consistent with a geometric or neo-grotesque sans. The upright Capitals subfamily and strong weight range reinforce a display-capable grotesque character.
summary: A seven-weight grotesque with a dedicated Capitals subfamily, sharp forms and broad Latin language coverage across more than 300 languages.
description: Jozef offers seven weights from Thin to Heavy, each with a matching italic, plus a standalone Capitals cut suited to display and titling work. The family is built for clarity at large sizes, with sharp edges that give it a directness uncommon in softer grotesques. Language support is unusually wide, covering hundreds of Latin-script languages across Africa, the Pacific and the Americas, backed by a full OpenType feature set including oldstyle figures, fractions and scientific inferiors.
foundry_blurb: Underscore Type present Jozef as a family defined by sharp edges and a hard, clear voice, suited to settings where the message needs to land without ambiguity. The family includes fifteen styles plus a variable font, with a Capitals subfamily available separately and licensing options via Fontspring and MyFonts.

name: Constant
classification_notes: A clean, upright sans-serif with a wide weight range and no italics, consistent with a contemporary grotesque. The specimen language and structural approach suggest a general-purpose grotesque rather than geometric or humanist classification.
summary: A seven-weight grotesque sans with variable font support and an unusually broad Latin language coverage, suited to packaging and editorial work.
description: Spanning Thin to Heavy with a variable font option, Constant covers a wide tonal range without italic variants. Its character set extends well beyond Western European Latin, supporting Vietnamese and a large number of minority and regional languages. OpenType features include ligatures, fractions, scientific inferiors, superscripts and two stylistic sets for alternate J and R forms.
foundry_blurb: Underscore Type present Constant as a versatile sans suited to bold statements, packaging and fashion applications, with a full weight range from Thin to Heavy including a variable font, and comprehensive multilingual Latin support.

name: Quell
classification_notes: Quell is a sans-serif with two distinct subfamilies: a monolinear cut (Linear) and a stroke-contrasted cut (Contrast), both sharing humanist proportions suited to identity and editorial use.
summary: A sans-serif in two structural variants — monolinear and stroke-contrasted — spanning six weights with obliques, designed for identity, signage and editorial work.
description: Quell splits into two subfamilies: Linear, with even stroke widths for direct and imperative messaging, and Contrast, where stroke variation lends a warmer, more expressive character. Both run through six weights from Thin to Extrabold, each with a matching oblique, giving 24 static styles plus a variable font. Latin script coverage is extensive, reaching several hundred languages.
foundry_blurb: Underscore Type describe Quell as having a Contrast subfamily, where stroke widths vary for a warmer and more humane voice, and a Linear subfamily with monowidth strokes suited to direct, imperative headlines and signage. They position it for identity, signage and sophisticated typographic contexts.

name: Silta
classification_notes: Designed with legibility and warmth as primary concerns, with true italics featuring distinctly shaped letterforms and curved entry and exit strokes on vertical stems, characteristics of humanist sans design.
summary: A humanist sans designed for interface legibility, with true italics and broad Latin script coverage across seven weights.
description: Built with legibility and warmth as its guiding principles, Silta targets interface typography where a human touch is needed without sacrificing clarity. Its italics are true italics: the 'a' and 'f' take distinctly italic forms rather than simply slanting, and curved entry and exit strokes on vertical stems give the family a natural rhythm. Language coverage spans a very wide range of Latin-script languages, and OpenType features include proportional and tabular figures, fractions, ordinals, superscript, subscript and scientific inferiors.
foundry_blurb: Underscore Type describe Silta as a highly legible sans serif with warm character, suited to lending a human touch to interface typography. They note its broad Latin language coverage, range of typographic features and number sets, and true italics with distinctly shaped letter variants and curved stem terminals.

name: Silta
classification_notes: Described by the foundry as a highly legible sans serif with warm character and true italics with distinct letterform variants, placing it firmly in the humanist sans tradition suited to interface and text use.
summary: A legible humanist sans with warm character, designed with interface typography in mind and extensive Latin script language coverage.
description: Fourteen styles span seven weights from Thin to Heavy, each with a matching true italic where characters such as 'a' and 'f' take on distinct forms rather than simply slanting. Curved entry and exit strokes on vertical stems give the italics a natural, handwritten quality. The character set covers a very large range of Latin-script languages alongside a full suite of OpenType features including multiple numeral sets, fractions and scientific notation.
foundry_blurb: Underscore Type describe Silta as a highly legible sans serif conceived to bring a human touch to interface typography. It offers broad language coverage for major Latin-script languages alongside a range of typographic features, number sets and icons.

name: Jozef
classification_notes: The specimen copy and sample settings suggest a clean, sharp-edged sans with strong geometric construction, no serifs, and a direct, modernist voice consistent with geometric sans classification.
summary: A sharp-edged geometric sans in seven weights with matching italics, a dedicated Capitals subfamily and a variable font, covering an exceptionally broad range of Latin-script languages.
description: Seven weights from Thin to Heavy, each with a matching italic, give Jozef a full palette for text and display use. A separate Capitals style and a variable font are also available. Language support is unusually extensive, running to several hundred Latin-script languages including minority and indigenous languages across multiple continents.
foundry_blurb: Underscore Type position Jozef as a family with a sharp edge and a clear voice, available in individual styles or as a complete bundle, with licensing options through the foundry directly or via Fontspring and MyFonts.
